name: Tortelli Di Zucca (Pumpkin Tortelli) l a Time
recipeIngredient: 1 3/4 lb Yellow pumpkin or Hubbard squash (or Acorn)
1/4 lb Mostarda di Cremona (fruit pickled in mustard), finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the filling up to 2 days in advance, but definitely before rolling out the pasta.
2. Cut pumpkin or squash into sections and remove seeds. Bake at 350°F until tender.
3. Pass through a food mill into a large bowl.
4. Add Mostarda, amaretti, cheese, and nutmeg. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Mix well; taste for mustard heat. If needed, add up to 1 tsp dried mustard.
6. The filling should be firm enough to hold together when formed into balls.
7. If not, add up to 2 tbsp bread crumbs.
8. In a bowl, mix flour and beaten eggs. Knead into a smooth ball and wrap in plastic.
9. Let rest for at least 1/2 hour.
10. Divide dough into quarters; work with one portion at a time, keeping the rest tightly wrapped.
11. Roll out on the pasta machine to the next-to-thinnest setting.
12. Cut into long strips, then cut at 2-inch intervals into squares.
13. Place a ball of stuffing slightly smaller than a walnut in the center of each rectangle.
14. Diagonally fold over one corner of the rectangle to meet the opposite corner.
15. Press to seal; fold the base of the triangle to form a "paper hat" shape and press sides to seal.
16. Repeat until all dough is used up.
17. Heat serving dish with 1 1/2 tbsp cheese.
18. Melt butter in a saucepan and cook tortelli in abundant, salted water for 4-5 minutes until cooked.
19. Remove with a slotted spoon, careful not to break the pasta.
20. Place tortelli over cheese, sprinkle with another 1 1/2 tbsp cheese, pour melted butter over, and toss gently.
21. Makes 8 servings.
Each serving contains about: 274 calories; 115 mg sodium; 105 mg cholesterol; 11 grams fat; 37 grams carbs.; 8 grams protein; 1.44 grams fiber. (Manny Rothstein notes: The ratio of eggs to flour may need adjustment for desired texture, and if the filling needs more heat, consider using Japanese horseradish.)
